request.question: Are both Dave Evans and Heidi Newfield both singers?

title: Burns & Poe
Trick Pony
List of AC/DC band members

sentences: Burns & Poe was an American country music duo composed of Keith Burns and Michelle Poe.
The duo was founded in 2007 after Burns left the group Trick Pony.

Poe was once a solo artist for DreamWorks Nashville, and later played bass guitar for Dierks Bentley and Hank Williams, Jr. The two met in 2007 while Burns was holding auditions to recruit a new lead singer for Trick Pony after Aubrey Collins (who had replaced original lead singer Heidi Newfield just months earlier) left.

Trick Pony is an American country music group, formed in 1996 by Heidi Newfield (lead vocals, acoustic guitar, harmonica), Keith Burns (lead guitar, vocals), and Ira Dean (bass guitar, upright bass, vocals).
They recorded three studio albums: "Trick Pony", "On a Mission", and "R.I.D.E.", released in 2001, 2002, and 2005.
These albums produced eight singles on the "Billboard" Hot Country Songs chart, including four Top 20 hits: "Pour Me", "On a Night like This", "Just What I Do", and "On a Mission".
AC/DC are an Australian hard rock band formed by guitarist brothers Angus and Malcolm Young in 1973.

Originally featuring vocalist Dave Evans, bassist Larry Van Kriedt and drummer Colin Burgess, after a number of lineup changes the band settled with Bon Scott on vocals, Mark Evans on bass guitar and Phil Rudd on drums, and released five albums (two limited to Australia) between 1974 and 1977.

Due to continuing clashes with lead guitarist Angus Young, Evans was later fired from the band shortly after the release of "Let There Be Rock", although "musical differences" was the official reason given for his departure.
Cliff Williams was brought in as his replacement.
"Pour Me" is a debut song recorded by American country music group Trick Pony.
It was released in October 2000 as the first single from their debut album "Trick Pony".
The song was written by group members Keith Burns, Ira Dean and Heidi Newfield with Rory Waters Beighley and Sammy Harp Wedlock.
"Johnny & June" is the solo debut song co-written and recorded by American country music singer Heidi Newfield, the former lead singer of the group Trick Pony.
It was released in March 2008 as the first single from her debut album "What Am I Waiting For", which was released in August 2008 on Curb Records.
The song reached a peak of #11 on the Hot Country Songs charts in late September 2008.
Heidi Newfield (born October 4, 1970) is an American country music artist.
She was lead singer, rhythm guitarist and harmonica player for the group Trick Pony, alongside Keith Burns and Ira Dean from 1996 until 2006, when she left in pursuit of a solo career.
Newfield has begun her solo career on Curb Records, debuting in 2008 with the single, "Johnny & June."

This song, which peaked at No. 11 on the Hot Country Songs charts, is the first release from her solo debut album, "What Am I Waiting For", which has produced a second Top 30 country hit, "Cry Cry ('Til the Sun Shines)."
"Cry Cry ('Til the Sun Shines)" is a song originally recorded by Martina McBride on her 2007 album "Waking Up Laughing".
It was later recorded by American country music artist Heidi Newfield and released in November 2008 as the second single from the album "What Am I Waiting For".
The song reached #29 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Songs chart.
The song was written by Marv Green, Aimee Mayo, Chris Lindsey and Hillary Lindsey.
Dave Evans (born 20 July 1953) is an Australian singer who briefly sang for the hard rock band AC/DC in 1973–1974.
What Am I Waiting For is the debut solo album from American country music singer Heidi Newfield, formerly the lead vocalist in the group Trick Pony.
It was released on August 5, 2008 on Curb Records and produced by Tony Brown.
The album's lead-off single, "Johnny & June", peaked at number 11 on the Hot Country Songs charts.
This song was followed by "Cry, Cry ('Til the Sun Shines)", which peaked at number 29.
"What Am I Waiting For" debuted on the "Billboard" 200 at number 10 with 34,000 copies sold in its first week.

Stephen "Steve" Nemesh (31 December 1896 in Szécsény, Hungary – 18 September 1975 in San Bernardino, California) was a Hungarian-born open-wheel racecar driver, who emigrated to the United States and competed as an American.
His only American Championship car racing start was the 1926 Indianapolis 500 where he drove an Argyle powered Schmidt chassis.
He started 22nd and was sidelined after completing 41 laps by transmission failure.
He was credited with 21st place.
He drove as a relief driver in the 1927 Indianapolis 500 for Dave Evans.

- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.

Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.

candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Are both Dave Evans and Heidi Newfield both singers?
hop1_docs: Heidi Newfield | Heidi Newfield (born October 4, 1970) is an American country music artist.  She was lead singer, rhythm guitarist and harmonica player for the group Trick Pony, alongside Keith Burns and Ira Dean from 1996 until 2006, when she left in pursuit of a solo career.  Newfield has begun her solo career on Curb Records, debuting in 2008 with the single, "Johnny & June."  This song, which peaked at No. 11 on the Hot Country Songs charts, is the first release from her solo debut album, "What Am I Waiting For", which has produced a second Top 30 country hit, "Cry Cry ('Til the Sun Shines)."
David Evans (rugby) | David "Dave" Alexander Evans (4 October 1886 – 12 October 1940) was a New Zealand dual-code international who represented his country in both rugby union and rugby league.
Dave Evans (singer) | Dave Evans (born 20 July 1953) is an Australian singer who briefly sang for the hard rock band AC/DC in 1973–1974.

Johnny & June (song) | "Johnny & June" is the solo debut song co-written and recorded by American country music singer Heidi Newfield, the former lead singer of the group Trick Pony.  It was released in March 2008 as the first single from her debut album "What Am I Waiting For", which was released in August 2008 on Curb Records.  The song reached a peak of #11 on the Hot Country Songs charts in late September 2008.

Killing Heidi (album) | Killing Heidi is the third album by Australian rock band Killing Heidi, released by Columbia Records in Australia on 30 August 2004 (see 2004 in music).  It was the band's first album without their producer Paul Kosky—who worked on their previous albums and has a more "sophisticated and grittier" feel to it with the lead singer, Ella Hooper, stating that releasing the album is "just like a re-launch".  "Killing Heidi" debuted in the top ten on the Australian ARIA Albums Chart, making it their second top ten album and sales were similar to those of Killing Heidi's second album, "Present" (2002) – both were certified gold by ARIA for 35000 units shipped.  Their third album gave Killing Heidi another top twenty single with "I Am", but its second single, "Calm Down" was not a major hit.

Rachel Bay Jones | Rachel Bay Jones (born November 8, 1969) is an American stage actress and singer.  She has played the roles of Catherine in the 2013 Broadway revival of "Pippin" and Evan's mother Heidi in "Dear Evan Hansen".  The latter earned her the 2017 Tony Award for Best Featured Actress in a Musical.

Tiffany Evans discography | American singer and songwriter Tiffany Evans has released one studio album, two extended plays/mixtapes, nine singles, two featured singles and five promotional singles. Evans appeared on "Star Search" in February 2003, where she won the Grand Champion title in junior singer division.  She competed against fellow singers Lisa Tucker and David Archuleta, who both later appeared on "American Idol".  In the same year she signed to Columbia Records.
response.summary_1:
Primary topics: Heidi Newfield and Dave/David Evans (name variants; singer identity).

- Heidi Newfield — "born October 4, 1970"; described as "an American country music artist"; "She was lead singer" of Trick Pony "from 1996 until 2006"; solo debut single "Johnny & June" released in 2008.
- Dave Evans (singer) — "Dave Evans (born 20 July 1953) is an Australian singer" who "briefly sang for the hard rock band AC/DC in 1973–1974."
- Alternate/ambiguous candidate — David "Dave" Alexander Evans (4 October 1886 – 12 October 1940), a New Zealand rugby player (not a singer) is also present in the passages (name variant: David/Dave Evans).
- Ambiguity/missing facts: the name "Dave/David Evans" is ambiguous between at least two individuals; passages do contain an explicit singer entry for "Dave Evans (born 20 July 1953)" and for Heidi Newfield, so no additional retrieval needed to establish whether those two specific entries are described as singers.
- Prioritized next-hop retrievals (if verification or disambiguation desired):
  1. "Dave Evans (singer)" — inspect lede/infobox and "Career" section to confirm singer role and dates.
  2. "Heidi Newfield" — inspect lede/infobox and "Career" (or "Biography") section for singer/lead-singer details.
  3. "David Evans (disambiguation)" or "David Evans (rugby)" — to confirm distinct non-singer individual and disambiguate name variants.

hop2_docs: Heidi Newfield | Heidi Newfield (born October 4, 1970) is an American country music artist.  She was lead singer, rhythm guitarist and harmonica player for the group Trick Pony, alongside Keith Burns and Ira Dean from 1996 until 2006, when she left in pursuit of a solo career.  Newfield has begun her solo career on Curb Records, debuting in 2008 with the single, "Johnny & June."  This song, which peaked at No. 11 on the Hot Country Songs charts, is the first release from her solo debut album, "What Am I Waiting For", which has produced a second Top 30 country hit, "Cry Cry ('Til the Sun Shines)."
Dave Evans (singer) | Dave Evans (born 20 July 1953) is an Australian singer who briefly sang for the hard rock band AC/DC in 1973–1974.

Johnny & June (song) | "Johnny & June" is the solo debut song co-written and recorded by American country music singer Heidi Newfield, the former lead singer of the group Trick Pony.  It was released in March 2008 as the first single from her debut album "What Am I Waiting For", which was released in August 2008 on Curb Records.  The song reached a peak of #11 on the Hot Country Songs charts in late September 2008.

Rachel Bay Jones | Rachel Bay Jones (born November 8, 1969) is an American stage actress and singer.  She has played the roles of Catherine in the 2013 Broadway revival of "Pippin" and Evan's mother Heidi in "Dear Evan Hansen".  The latter earned her the 2017 Tony Award for Best Featured Actress in a Musical.

Burns & Poe | Burns & Poe was an American country music duo composed of Keith Burns and Michelle Poe.  The duo was founded in 2007 after Burns left the group Trick Pony.  Poe was once a solo artist for DreamWorks Nashville, and later played bass guitar for Dierks Bentley and Hank Williams, Jr. The two met in 2007 while Burns was holding auditions to recruit a new lead singer for Trick Pony after Aubrey Collins (who had replaced original lead singer Heidi Newfield just months earlier) left.

Dave Evans (bluegrass) | William "Dave" Evans (July 24, 1950 - June 26, 2017) was a tenor singer, banjo player, composer, and bluegrass band leader.  He was noted for his powerful tenor vocal range and for his style which bridged traditional and contemporary bluegrass.  Notable songs written by Evans include "One Loaf of Bread," "Highway 52," "99 Years is Almost for Life," and "Be Proud of the Grey in Your Hair."

What Am I Waiting For | What Am I Waiting For is the debut solo album from American country music singer Heidi Newfield, formerly the lead vocalist in the group Trick Pony.  It was released on August 5, 2008 on Curb Records and produced by Tony Brown.  The album's lead-off single, "Johnny & June", peaked at number 11 on the Hot Country Songs charts.  This song was followed by "Cry, Cry ('Til the Sun Shines)", which peaked at number 29.  "What Am I Waiting For" debuted on the "Billboard" 200 at number 10 with 34,000 copies sold in its first week.
response.summary_2: Yes — Heidi Newfield and Dave Evans are both singers. Heidi Newfield (born October 4, 1970) is an American country music artist who was lead singer of Trick Pony from 1996–2006 and launched a solo career with the 2008 single "Johnny & June." Dave Evans (born 20 July 1953) is an Australian singer who briefly sang with AC/DC in 1973–1974.
